urn:contentItem:6JGC-DM23-S9SW-31H9-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - NEWS AND TRENDS FROM PERKINS COIE AND ITS PEERS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:02:38ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - NEWS AND TRENDS FROM PERKINS COIE AND ITS PEERS

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [1 News That Matters]

+ MORGAN LEWIS EXPANDS BENEFITS TEAM WITH PILLSBURY HIRE

Morgan Lewis & Bockius LLP has announced the addition of a prominent attorney from Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP to its Employee Benefits team, a strategic step to address the growing complexities in benefits law and the demand for expert guidance.The attorney's multi-faceted background covers compliance and regulatory issues within employee benefits, and is expected to broaden the firm's capabilities in advising clients on benefit plans and pension laws.Arriving on 08th April 2026, the hire aligns with Morgan Lewis' broader push to strengthen its offerings in employee benefits amid regulatory shifts and an increasingly competitive legal market.Pillsbury has seen several of its attorneys move to other firms in recent years, and Morgan Lewis notes that the new hire will bring important regulatory insight to navigate evolving requirements.The firm has a longstanding reputation for depth in its practice areas, and leaders say the addition will help deliver more specialized services tailored to client needs.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 News That Matters]

+ ASHURST-PERKINS COIE MERGER CLEARED BY PARTNERS, CLOSE TARGET SET FOR Q3

Partner votes have backed the Ashurst and Perkins Coie merger, with the approval announced on 13th April 2026 and a closing target in the third quarter, subject to customary conditions.The move underscores a shared commitment to building a forward looking global firm and confidence in growth and in delivering premier legal services in a fast changing market.Paul Jenkins, CEO of Ashurst, said the two firms are well aligned and their combination will broaden client offerings and scale cross border capability.The combined group will number about 3,000 lawyers across 52 offices in 32 countries, with major hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney and New York, and a focus on technology, energy, infrastructure and financial services.The practice areas, together with a revenue base of around USD 2.8 billion, bring extensive international experience spanning Brussels, Dubai, Frankfurt, Hong Kong, Paris, Seoul, Shenzhen, Singapore and Tokyo. Leadership will be shared by Bill Malley and Paul Jenkins as global co-CEOs, with Ashurst global chair Karen Davies and Perkins Coie corporate practice chair Brian Eiting serving as co-chairs.The combination is expected to sharpen the firms' competitive edge and position the new entity for sustained growth in the evolving global legal market.

+ ASHURST AND PERKINS COIE AGREE TO STRATEGIC COMBINATION, LAUNCH ASHURST PERKINS COIE

NEW YORK — Perkins Coie and Ashurst announced today that the partnerships of both firms have voted overwhelmingly to approve their proposed combination, signaling broad support for a deal that pairs complementary geographic reach, sector depth, and practice strength. The date of this announcement is recorded as 2026-04-14 16:18:02.950901.Upon closing, Ashurst Perkins Coie will be a globally integrated firm with combined revenues of approximately US$2.8 billion and around 3,000 lawyers spread across more than 50 offices worldwide. The firm will run flagship hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney, and New York, underscoring its expansive footprint across key markets. It will be well positioned to serve clients in technology, energy and infrastructure, and financial services.Bill Malley, the Firmwide Managing Partner at Perkins Coie, described the vote as recognition that the merger will create a differentiated platform with scale, sector depth, and technology leadership to meet cross-border client needs. 'Our partnership's approval reflects a belief that combining to form Ashurst Perkins Coie will yield a differentiated global platform.'Paul Jenkins, Global CEO of Ashurst, stressed the alignment and ambition behind the move, noting that the combination will unlock complementary strengths across sectors and practice areas that will benefit clients. 'This vote confirms our joint ambition and the compelling platform it creates for top legal talent,' he said.The merger remains subject to customary closing conditions and is expected to finalize in the third quarter of 2026, with the new firm aiming to navigate the evolving global market and deliver enhanced services to clients.

+ PERKINS COIE RE-ESTABLISHES MAINLAND CHINA PRESENCE WITH SHENZHEN HUB

Perkins Coie is re-entering China with a Shenzhen representative office, inaugurated on Apr. 14, 2026, marking a renewed foothold in the market. The move follows the 2024 closure of its Beijing and Shanghai offices and signals a strategic pivot toward Shenzhen to tap the city's rapid growth as a tech and manufacturing hub. The office will be led by Bing Ai, chief representative and managing partner, and Wei Yuan, representative and litigation partner; both are US-trained and bring deep experience serving technology clients. Ai focuses on patent procurement and portfolio management in sectors such as semiconductors and medical devices, while Yuan handles patent infringement disputes. Bill Malley, Perkins Coie's managing partner, says the Shenzhen move underscores the firm's commitment to cross-border US legal expertise and helping clients navigate global markets amid rising cross-border challenges. The development comes as several international firms push deeper into Shenzhen's Greater Bay Area, and Perkins Coie weighs a potential merger with UK-based Ashurst that could create a top-20 global firm with projected annual revenues around $2.7 billion.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COOLEY LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ CROSBY RAISES $60 MILLION IN SERIES B LED BY LUX CAPITAL AND INDEX VENTURES, WITH COOLEY AMONG BACKERS

Crosby, the contract review start-up, announced it has closed a $60 million Series B on April 3, 2026, its third capital raise in under a year, with Cooley LLP among the backers.The round was co-led by Lux Capital and Index Ventures, with participation from Sequoia, 01 Advisors, Bain Capital Ventures and angel investor Elad Gil.The fresh capital will fund product development and help Crosby scale to serve a growing roster of clients, including Clay, Rogo and Ramp.Crosby uses artificial intelligence paired with legal expertise to accelerate contract reviews, and the company says the value of negotiated contracts now tops $1 billion, up from roughly $30 million when it emerged from stealth last year.In a blog post, Crosby stated, "As we’ve scaled, we’ve also started experimenting with entirely new technology experiences." The company has rolled out simulations of counterparty responses to contract redlines, voice agents that negotiate on clients’ behalf, and a client-facing platform to oversee Crosby’s work.Crosby was founded in 2024 by Ryan Daniels and John Sarihan. Daniels, a former Cooley associate, and Sarihan, a former Google software engineer and Ramp tech lead, have steered Crosby toward a future of faster contract negotiation with the support of investors including Cooley and other leading firms.

+ COOLEY TAPS FRESHFIELDS BRUCKHAUS DERINGER ALUMNI TO BOOST TECH PRACTICE

Cooley LLP is expanding its technology practice by recruiting several attorneys who previously worked at Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer, signaling a strategic effort to strengthen its tech capabilities and stay competitive in a dynamic legal market.As of 23rd November 2025, the firm’s latest recruitment drive highlights ongoing work to broaden technology offerings in a rapidly evolving sector. The addition of Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer alumni is anticipated to bolster the existing practice and help attract clients seeking specialized tech law guidance.The incoming team members bring broad experience, advising notable clients on IP, data protection, and corporate tech transactions. Their know-how is expected to augment Cooley’s well-established venture capital and startup advisory practice, further positioning the firm as a leading tech law player.The hire reflects a broader market trend toward poaching top talent to meet demands of a fast-paced, tech-driven environment. With tech-related legal challenges growing more complex, specialized knowledge is increasingly vital, making these hires timely.Through this expansion, Cooley seeks to reinforce its standing as a go-to tech law firm, supporting its innovation-driven growth. As integration proceeds, stakeholders will watch how these changes affect Cooley's market position and client services in the near term.

+ COOLEY LLP'S LITIGATION TEAM NAVIGATES A LEADERSHIP TEST AMID GROWTH

Cooley LLP’s litigation practice has been making headlines as a finalist for The American Lawyer's General Litigation Department of the Year. The firm, noted for its excellence, is currently confronting what partners describe as a 'test of leadership.' The practice, led by Ian Shapiro, is attending to critical challenges amid a changing legal landscape.The discussion about the firm's strategic direction occurred during a recent interview with Ian Shapiro, along with partners Michael Attanasio and Kathleen Hartnett, where they outlined the firm’s decision-making framework. They highlighted Cooley's representation of Jenner & Block in its high-profile bid against the administration, illustrating a commitment to impactful legal work. The interview appeared just 10 hours prior, providing a timely window into the firm's operations.In addition to Jenner & Block matters, Cooley has pursued a range of pro bono matters that reflect its devotion to public service and social justice. This focus has contributed to the firm's growth during the past year. As the litigation department grows, the firm is positioned to leverage its expertise in tackling complex legal challenges while remaining aligned with its core values.The growth of Cooley's litigation practice aligns with broader industry trends toward specialized services. This pivot enhances the firm's competitive edge and bodes well for future opportunities. As the fiscal year nears its end, stakeholders will closely monitor Cooley's next steps.As the 'test of leadership' plays out, the legal community and investors will be watching to see how Cooley navigates these challenges. The firm's proactive stance, together with a pursuit of excellence, suggests it may emerge stronger in a competitive market.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ REED SMITH LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ REED SMITH APPOINTS LONGTIME PHILADELPHIA EXECUTIVE TO LEAD LOCAL PRACTICE

ReedSmith LLP has named an internal partner to lead its Philadelphia office, underscoring a continuity-driven transition in a key market. The appointee has spent more than ten years at the firm and has played a pivotal role in high-profile cases, helping shape strategy for the Philadelphia footprint. The decision to promote from within aligns with the firm's strategy to cultivate internal talent while ensuring leadership continuity. The Philadelphia office is a core part of Reed Smith's national network, serving clients across sectors, and the new leader will prioritize strengthening core practice areas and expanding the firm's local footprint. Effective from 27th April 2026, the move signals optimism for future growth as the firm seeks to deepen client relationships and drive operational excellence in Philadelphia.

+ REED SMITH NAMES BOSTON THE SITE OF ITS INAUGURAL MANAGING PARTNER ROLE, SIGNALING GROWTH

ReedSmith LLP has appointed its first Managing Partner in Boston, signaling a new era for the firm in the Northeast market. Effective from 23rd April 2026, the appointment designates a local leader to head Boston operations and advance client development. The role is designed to strengthen relationships with current clients while pursuing new business opportunities across sectors where Boston has shown momentum. The firm noted that the decision follows an analysis of the region's burgeoning legal market and Reed Smith's broader expansion strategy in key markets. By tapping into local insights and forming partnerships aligned with its global vision, Reed Smith aims to embed itself more deeply into Boston's business landscape. The new Managing Partner brings a wealth of experience and a wide professional network that executives expect will raise operational efficiency and bolster the firm's reputation in the area. The Boston office is set to be a central pillar of the firm's growth plan as Reed Smith navigates the evolving legal market, with investors and stakeholders watching closely in the coming months.

+ REED SMITH BOLSTERS MIAMI TEAM WITH FUND FINANCE SPECIALIST FROM MCDERMOTT

ReedSmith LLP announced the recruitment of a senior fund finance professional from McDermott Will & Emery, boosting its Miami office and its capabilities in fund finance. The hire comes as the firm pursues a broader expansion plan in key markets. As of 17th April 2026, the move illustrates Reed Smith’s ongoing strategy to attract top talent in this specialized field. The recruited background includes extensive experience with banks, lenders and private equity sponsors on complex financing structures. The addition is expected to strengthen Reed Smith’s ability to serve a diverse client base of fund managers and investors, aligning with the firm’s aim to provide end-to-end legal support in fund finance transactions. Miami’s growing status as a financial hub has driven increased demand for fund finance advisory, and Reed Smith’s leadership says the hire will help the office meet that demand while advancing high quality service and innovation.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILSON SONSINI GOODRICH & ROSATI P.C. [3 News That Matters]

+ WILSON SONSINI APPOINTS BAKER MCKENZIE VETERAN AS CHIEF OPERATING OFFICER

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati P.C. has named a new Chief Operating Officer from Baker McKenzie, underscoring the firm's focus on strengthening operations. The identity of the incoming COO remains confidential for now, with the hire aimed at boosting efficiency and supporting growth in a competitive legal market.The announcement dated 22-04-2026 signals a strategic leadership update as Wilson Sonsini navigates industry changes and invests in technology and service excellence. The firm expects the new COO's experience in technology integration and client management to help streamline processes and enhance service delivery to clients.Wilson Sonsini's track record of technology focus and innovation aligns with the incoming executive's background in overseeing operational strategies at a large global firm. The leadership profile is seen as well suited to expanding the firm's presence in biotechnology, cybersecurity and intellectual property work.Market watchers welcomed the appointment, viewing it as a move to strengthen strategic direction and execution. Analysts say the hire could influence the firm's competitive position and future performance as it scales.

+ RAYTX CLOSES UPSIZED $125 MILLION SERIES B, BACKED BY NOVO HOLDINGS, NORWEST AND NEW INVESTORS

Ray Therapeutics (RayTx) closed a $125 million Series B that was upsized and oversubscribed, reflecting strong investor appetite for its vision restoration programs. The financing, announced on April 21, 2026, was led by Janus Henderson Investors with new participants including Adage Capital Management, Franklin Templeton, Invus, and Marshall Wace.Existing investors such as 4BIO Capital, Deerfield Management, and MRL Ventures Fund provided continued backing, while Novo Holdings A/S, Norwest, and Platanus joined as major supporters. An experienced law firm managed the advisory work for these backers to help structure the deal.The new capital will advance RayTx's late-stage clinical development and commercial readiness, focusing on RTx-015 for Retinitis Pigmentosa and RTx-021 for Stargardt Disease and Geographic Atrophy. The company said the advisory team for Novo Holdings A/S, Norwest, and Platanus included Dan Koeppen, Kassandra Castillo, and Nimit Dhir, highlighting the importance of strategic partnerships in clinical-scale biopharma.RayTx expects the funds to bolster its pathway to market and support broader ophthalmology trials, with coverage of its lead and follow-on assets. The company also pointed readers to its official news release and industry coverage from Fierce Biotech and Pulse 2.0 for more details.

+ EPP SECURES PRO BONO LEGAL SUPPORT FOR P-REC MINI-GRID FINANCING PILOT

On March 25, 2026, the African Development Bank Group and Nordic Development Fund announced a $11.3 million commitment to pilot the Peace Renewable Energy Certificate (P-REC) Aggregation Facility, the first deployment of renewable energy certificates as a direct funding mechanism for a portfolio of mini-grids across 14 African countries.The facility will be co-managed by Camco Clean Energy, recognized for its climate and impact investment strategies, and Energy Peace Partners (EPP), a coalition dedicated to leveraging climate finance solutions.The initiative is expected to establish long-term purchase agreements with mini-grid developers in Burundi, Ethiopia, and Nigeria, enabling electricity access for approximately 856,000 people.The legal support to EPP during this transaction was pro bono, provided by a team including Scott Zimmermann, Nadia Senter, and Ethan Miller, reflecting the firm's commitment to renewables.By aggregating renewable energy certificates, the P-REC Aggregation Facility aims to create a sustainable financial model that empowers local developers and advances climate resilience and energy equity; details are in the AFDB announcement.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ TELIX-REGENERON RADIOPHARMACEUTICAL PACT VALUED UP TO USD 2.1 BILLION; SIDLEY AUSTIN ADVISES TELIX

Sidley Austin is advising Telix Pharmaceuticals on its strategic collaboration with Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, a partnership valued at up to USD 2.1 billion. The arrangement will see Telix and Regeneron co-developing and co-commercialising next-generation radiopharmaceutical therapies under a 50:50 cost-and-profit-sharing model, with Telix receiving USD 40 million upfront for four initial programmes. Disclosed on 21st April 2026, the deal centers on joint governance and shared development and commercialization responsibilities, aiming to leverage both companies' oncology platforms. Telix is pursuing an aggressive expansion in radiopharmaceuticals, seeking to broaden its footprint in cancer care through partnerships that combine Regeneron's oncology capabilities with Telix's radiopharmaceutical pipeline. Joshua Hofheimer, a Sidley partner, commented that "These considerations affect governance, regulatory matters, and intellectual property, in particular." The alliance is positioned to accelerate radiopharmaceutical development while balancing regulatory and commercial considerations. Industry observers note that the deal aligns with a broader wave of M&A activity in the biopharma space, and Sidley Austin continues to build its reputation as a premier adviser on cross-border collaborations in a changing regulatory environment.

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN EXPANDS CITY FOOTPRINT AS REVENUE RISES 33% ON HIRING DRIVE

Sidley Austin is reporting a 33% increase in City revenue, credited to a targeted hiring push as of 14th April 2026. The growth underscores Sidley's expanded City footprint following recruitment of senior lawyers and other specialists. Executives say the hires have diversified practice areas and improved client service, lifting billable hours and retention. Leadership intends to refine hiring practices and broaden capabilities to stay ahead in a competitive market. Industry observers see the firm's approach as a potential model for other firms navigating tighter margins and evolving client needs.

+ CFTC TAPS SIDLEY AND LATHAM TO SHAPE FORWARD-LOOKING RULES FOR DIGITAL DERIVATIVES

The CFTC has established an Innovation Task Force by engaging SidleyAustin and Latham & Watkins to advance the regulatory framework for the derivatives markets, aligning oversight with rapid technological change.The agency announced on 11th April 2026 that Sidley Austin, with deep regulatory experience, and Latham & Watkins, providing strategic counsel, will help translate market developments into robust policy.Sidley Austin's team is expected to offer guidance on best practices as the derivatives space integrates technologies such as blockchain and artificial intelligence.The task force forms part of a broader CFTC strategy to both regulate and facilitate innovation, seeking to balance risk management with opportunities for market participants to deploy new tools.Industry stakeholders will watch for potential policy shifts and how the collaboration could influence regulatory expectations, with implications for U.S. competitiveness in global markets.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG EXPANDS LONDON REAL ESTATE FINANCE CAPABILITIES AMID STRONG YEAR

Greenberg Traurig, LLP's London Real Estate Finance team has advised on transactions with a combined value exceeding £5 billion across asset classes, clients and financing structures, a milestone reached on the 23rd of April 2026. Promotions within the practice reflect ongoing growth: William Lo has been promoted to shareholder and Priyanka Verma elevated to senior associate, bringing the team to four shareholders and nine attorneys. The team was named a finalist for Real Estate Team of the Year at The Lawyer Awards 2026 for its advisory on the £1.1 billion take-private of The PRS REIT plc, with the winner to be announced on 16 June 2026, and it is also in contention for Lawyer of the Year at the PROPS Awards on 14 May 2026. Duncan Hubbard, chair of the London Real Estate Finance team, said the strength of the market continues to present compelling opportunities and that the record year reflects both market conditions and the trust clients place in the team. With around 3,100 lawyers across 51 locations, Greenberg Traurig is recognised for innovative legal services and pro bono efforts, reinforcing its position in the legal landscape. The firm has consistently ranked among the Am Law Global 100 and NLJ500.

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG NAMED FINALIST IN CHAMBERS ENVIRONMENT AND REAL ESTATE CATEGORIES FOR 2026

Greenberg Traurig, LLP, a global law firm, has been shortlisted by Chambers for the 2026 Environment Law Firm of the Year and Real Estate Law Firm of the Year awards in its Environmental and Real Estate practices. Chambers announced the shortlists on 23rd April 2026, with winners to be revealed at a ceremony on 11th June 2026 at The Plaza Hotel in New York.The firm is among six finalists in each category, a marker of Greenberg Traurig's sustained performance. Chambers notes the awards recognize outstanding legal work, strategic growth, and client service over the past year, and their nominations result from Chambers' research by more than 250 analysts.The Environmental and Real Estate teams are praised for guiding clients through complex challenges with innovative, business-driven strategies. Greenberg Traurig's global platform—about 3,100 lawyers across 51 offices—supports cross-border service across industries, a factor that has contributed to its 2025 BTI Best of the Best Recommended Law Firm for trust and relationships.As the firm continues to build its reputation for excellence, the Chambers nomination highlights its ongoing commitment to top-tier legal services. The legal community awaits the awards' outcome, expected to spotlight firms at the industry’s forefront.

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG CLINCHES TOP ANTITRUST AWARD AT LEGAL 500 MEXICO AWARDS 2026

Apr. 23, 2026: Greenberg Traurig's Mexico City office has been named Competition and Antitrust Firm of the Year at the Legal 500 Mexico Awards 2026, announced in Mexico City last week. The recognition rests on Legal 500's independent research for its annual directory. Jose Raz Guzman, co-chair of the Latin America Practice and co-managing shareholder of the Mexico City office, said the accolade reflects strong leadership and deep knowledge within the Antitrust Litigation & Competition Regulation Practice. The firm’s team, led by Miguel Flores Bernes, Victor Manuel Frias and Rocio Olea Salgado, is recognized for advising on antitrust aspects of mergers, acquisitions and joint ventures, and for handling complex multijurisdictional merger control filings. It is supported by associates Valery Garcia Zavala, Ana Ramirez, Natalia Mejia and Martin Lopez. Beyond merger work, Greenberg Traurig provides compliance guidance, internal investigations and proceedings before competition authorities. The Mexico City office houses more than 70 bilingual attorneys and plays a central role in the firm’s Latin America Practice. With over 3,100 lawyers across 51 locations, Greenberg Traurig leverages global resources to deliver strategic legal services, and the firm has long been ranked in Am Law Global 100, NLJ500 and Law360 400.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GOODWIN PROCTER LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ FBT GIBBONS ADDS VENTURE CAPITAL ATTORNEY FROM GOODWIN PROCTER LLP

On 21st April 2026, FBT Gibbons confirmed the recruitment of a venture capital attorney from Goodwin Procter LLP to its New Jersey office. The hire strengthens the firm's venture capital practice as the sector grows. The incoming attorney brings broad experience from Goodwin Procter LLP, known for its robust venture capital work spanning startups and established companies. FBT Gibbons says the addition will help clients raise capital and navigate complex investment environments. The move reflects a wider industry trend of firms expanding niche capabilities to serve clients in New Jersey and beyond.

+ TOPSPIN CONSUMER PARTNERS CLOSES $328 MILLION FUND WITH LEGAL SUPPORT FROM GOODWIN PROCTER

Goodwin Procter LLP supported Topspin Consumer Partners in closing its latest fundraising effort, securing $328 million for a consumer-focused fund. The achievement marks a notable development in the private equity landscape, with Goodwin Procter providing essential counsel throughout the process.On April 15, 2026, Topspin announced the fund’s close, signaling an intent to capitalize on opportunities within the consumer sector. The firm’s legal team helped navigate complex fundraising dynamics, ensure regulatory compliance, and streamline commitments from potential investors.The fund will pursue a wide range of opportunities across consumer markets, with a focus on sectors showing robust growth. Through Goodwin Procter’s support, Topspin Consumer Partners seeks to identify and invest in companies well-positioned to perform in a changing market environment.The collaboration highlights Goodwin Procter’s reputation in private equity and its ongoing commitment to helping clients achieve their investment objectives, potentially boosting investor confidence as the fund scales.

+ GOODWIN PROCTER ADOPTS AI TOOLS TO BOOST EFFICIENCY IN PRIVATE EQUITY AND TECH PRACTICE

Goodwin Procter LLP is joining a wave of BigLaw firms adopting AI-native approaches to legal practice, building on its strength in private equity and technology law. The firm's emphasis on efficiency and client service reflects a broader industry shift toward AI-enabled workflows.On 10th April 2026, the firm publicly outlined its plan to weave AI into its workflows, signaling leadership in a field undergoing rapid technological change.The AI tools are expected to reshape research, document review and case-management analytics, reducing routine work and enabling lawyers to tackle more intricate matters.This move reflects a broader industry shift as clients seek faster, more efficient service, and as peers pursue similar AI-driven strategies.Industry observers see Goodwin Procter as positioning itself to raise service standards and maintain a competitive edge amid ongoing disruption.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ NICOLE LOPEZ JOINS ORRICK TO DRIVE YOUTH ONLINE SAFETY STRATEGY AND REGULATION

Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe announced the addition of Nicole Lopez, a recognized authority in youth online safety policy and litigation, to its team. Lopez previously held a senior role at Meta, underscoring her capability to navigate complex safety issues as the digital environment evolves.On Apr. 28, 2026, Lopez expressed enthusiasm for the new role and the opportunity to broaden her impact in the field.Her background spans policy, litigation and regulatory strategy, which the firm says will help clients facing increasing scrutiny over online safety. Jeremy Kudon, who leads Orrick's State Legislative practice, said, "We are facing a watershed moment. Online safety is top of mind for innovators, government, teens and parents, and society at large." The firm notes that 25 state legislatures are actively considering legislation aimed at safeguarding minors in digital spaces.Aravind Swaminathan, Orrick's Tech Sector Co-Leader, called Lopez a unique blend of skills—"part policy expert, part litigation and regulatory strategist, and part business advisor"—and said she will be an extraordinary resource for clients as AI reshapes the digital landscape. The Online Safety Law Center will leverage her leadership to keep clients informed of rapid developments and to advocate for policies that balance innovation with child protection.

+ ORRICK BOLSTERS DIGITAL SAFETY TEAM WITH META VETERAN

On Apr. 23, 2026, Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe announced the appointment of the former Meta youth safety policy head, adding depth to its digital security and regulatory practice.The incoming advisor brings a robust background in policy formulation and implementation, with specific focus on safeguarding young users in digital environments.Officials say the hire will help Orrick advise clients on a broad set of issues, including privacy protections, data privacy regulations, and compliance obligations tied to online youth safety.The recruitment reflects Orrick's broader strategy to stay ahead of regulatory developments and position itself as a leading adviser on technology-related risk for a changing legal landscape.Industry observers expect the appointment to enhance the firm's ability to help clients navigate complex rules as platforms face increasing scrutiny over practices affecting minors.

+ ORRICK TAPS CADWALDER VETERANS TO STRENGTHEN LONDON FINANCE PRACTICE

Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe has hired Suzanne Bell and William Bibby from Cadwalader for its London office, bringing the total Cadwalader hires to 42 since last year’s drive. Bell is a top-ranked securitisation partner who spent more than 13 years at Cadwalader, while Bibby, a special counsel, will become a partner after joining from Linklaters a year ago. These additions come as part of Orrick’s September 2025 expansion of its structured finance practice, which formed a transatlantic CLO and asset-backed lending team across London and the US, totalling 37 lawyers, including 10 partners. Orrick's chair Mitch Zuklie described the launch as a 'game-changer' for the firm. The early impact is already being felt. In Q1 2026, Orrick was the second most active arranger counsel for CLO deals globally, and its London team advised on four European private credit CLOs during the quarter. Global structured finance group leader Leah Sanzari said the group’s performance has exceeded expectations as the firm continues to push into innovative product areas. The hiring spree highlights Orrick’s aggressive market positioning as the CLO market grows in tandem with private credit, with rising demand for 'middle-market' CLOs backed by private debt funds. Cadwalader’s departures come as the firm gears up for a Hogan Lovells merger, with a planned 1 July launch.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 Press Releases]

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC MAMMOTH FREIGHTERS RECEIVES FAA CERTIFICATION FOR 777-200LRMF FREIGHTER

Fort Worth, Texas - April 8, 2026 - Mammoth Freighters LLC today announced it has received certification from the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) for its 777-200LRMF (Long Range Mammoth Freighter), marking a major milestone in the company's expansion of next-generation widebody freighter solutions.

FAA certification validates the aircraft's design, engineering, and performance, clearing the 777-200LRMF for commercial service. The platform delivers a compelling combination of long-range capability, payload efficiency, and operational reliability, positioning it as a highly versatile solution for global cargo networks.

Source: Company Website

+ BROOKFIELD ASSET MANAGEMENT LTD [3 Press Releases]

+ BROOKFIELD ASSET MANAGEMENT ANNOUNCES PRICING OF $550 MILLION OF NOTES DUE 2031 AND $450 MILLION RE-OPENING OF NOTES DUE 2036

NEW YORK, April 14, 2026 -- Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. (BAM) (NYSE: BAM, TSX: BAM), a leading global alternative asset manager headquartered in New York with over $1 trillion of assets under management, today priced an offering of (i) $550 million principal amount of senior notes due 2031, which will bear interest at a rate of 4.832% per annum (the 2031 notes), and (ii) $450 million re-opening of its 5.298% notes due 2036 (the new 2036 notes and, together with the 2031 notes, the notes).

The new 2036 notes will form part of the same series as the already outstanding $400 million principal amount of 5.298% notes due 2036 (the existing 2036 notes), which were issued on November 18, 2025. After giving effect to the re-opening, the aggregate principal amount of the series will be $850 million. The terms of the new 2036 notes will be identical to the existing 2036 notes, other than the issue date and the issue price. The new 2036 notes will be issued at a price equal to 98.962% of their principal value plus accrued and unpaid interest from November 18, 2025 (the issue date of the existing 2036 notes) through, but excluding, the date of delivery of such notes, with an effective yield of 5.434%, if held to maturity.

The net proceeds from the sale of the notes will be used for general corporate purposes. The offering is expected to close on April 17, 2026, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.

The notes are being offered pursuant to prospectus supplements to BAMs existing base shelf prospectus filed in the United States and Canada and pursuant to an effective registration statement on Form F-10 on file with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Copies of the prospectus supplement and accompanying base shelf prospectus may be obtained free of charge on EDGAR at www.sec.gov/edgar or on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca. Before you invest, you should read these documents and other public filings by BAM for more complete information about BAM and this offering. Alternatively, copies can be obtained from the joint book-running managers and underwriters:

Source: West Corporation

+ BROOKFIELD ASSET MANAGEMENT ANNOUNCES $1 BILLION COMMERCIAL PAPER PROGRAM

NEW YORK, March 03, 2026 -- Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. (NYSE: BAM) (TSX: BAM) (Brookfield) announced today that it has established a commercial paper program on a private placement basis. Under the program, Brookfield may issue unsecured commercial paper notes (the notes) up to a maximum aggregate amount outstanding at any time of $1 billion. The program is expected to further diversify and strengthen Brookfields balance sheet by providing another source of short-term capital. The proceeds from the issuance of any notes will be used for general corporate purposes.

The notes to be offered under the program have not been and will not be registered under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the Securities Act), and may not be offered or sold in the United States absent registration or an applicable exemption from registration requirements of the Securities Act and applicable state laws. This press release shall not constitute an offer to sell or the solicitation of an offer to buy notes under Brookfield Asset Managements commercial paper program and is being issued in accordance with Rule 135c under the Securities Act.

About Brookfield Asset Management

Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. (NYSE: BAM, TSX: BAM) is a leading global alternative asset manager, headquartered in New York, with over $1 trillion of assets under management across renewable power and transition, infrastructure, private equity, real estate, and credit. We invest client capital for the long-term with a focus on real assets and essential service businesses that form the backbone of the global economy. We offer a range of alternative investment products to investors around the world including public and private pension plans, endowments and foundations, sovereign wealth funds, financial institutions, insurance companies and private wealth investors. We draw on Brookfields heritage as an owner and operator to invest for value and generate strong returns for our clients, across economic cycles.

For more information, please visit our website at www.bam.brookfield.com

Source: West Corporation

+ BROOKFIELD ASSET MANAGEMENT ANNOUNCES RENEWAL OF NORMAL COURSE ISSUER BID

NEW YORK, Jan. 09, 2026 -- Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. (NYSE: BAM, TSX: BAM) (Brookfield Asset Management) today announced it has received the requisite approval for the renewal of its normal course issuer bid providing the option to purchase up to 36,946,177 Class A Limited Voting Shares (Class A Shares), representing approximately 10% of the public float of Brookfield Asset Managements outstanding Class A Shares. Purchases under the bid will be made on the open market through the facilities of the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE), Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX), and/or alternative trading systems. The period of the normal course issuer bid will extend from January 13, 2026 to January 12, 2027, or an earlier date should Brookfield Asset Management complete its purchases. Brookfield Asset Management will pay the market price at the time of acquisition for any Class A Shares purchased or such other price as may be permitted.

As at December 31, 2025, the number of Class A Shares issued and outstanding totaled 1,637,941,906 of which 369,461,770 shares represented the public float. The maximum daily purchase on the TSX under this bid will be 365,499 Class A Shares, which is 25% of 1,461,999 (the average daily trading volume for Class A Shares on the TSX for the six months ended December 31, 2025).

Of the 37,123,295 Class A Shares approved for purchase under Brookfield Asset Managements prior normal course issuer bid that commenced on January 13, 2025 and will expire on January 12, 2026, Brookfield Asset Management purchased 6,548,561 Class A Shares as of December 31, 2025; 100,000 Class A Shares through open market purchases on the TSX and 6,448,561 Class A Shares through open market purchases on the NYSE and/or alternative trading systems. The weighted average price that Brookfield Asset Management paid per Class A Share acquired under this bid was US$54.14.

Brookfield Asset Management is renewing its normal course issuer bid to retain the option to acquire its Class A Shares where this aligns with its investment and capital allocation strategies. All Class A Shares acquired by Brookfield Asset Management under this bid will be cancelled and/or purchased by a non-independent trustee pursuant to the terms of Brookfield Asset Managements long-term incentive plans.

Brookfield Asset Management also announced that it has entered into an automatic purchase plan in relation to the normal course issuer bid. The automatic purchase plan allows for the potential purchase of Class A Shares during the term of the normal course issuer bid, subject to certain trading parameters, at times when Brookfield Asset Management ordinarily would not be active in the market due to its own internal trading black-out period, insider trading rules or otherwise. Outside of these periods, Class A Shares will be repurchased in accordance with managements discretion and in compliance with applicable law.

About Brookfield Asset Management

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. [3 Press Releases]

+ CARLYLE GROUP CENTERRA GOLD ANNOUNCES EXECUTIVE CHANGES

TORONTO, March 30, 2026 -- Centerra Gold Inc. (Centerra or the Company) (TSX: CG) (NYSE: CGAU) announces that David Hendriks, Executive Vice President and Chief Operating Officer (COO), will be leaving the Company. He will remain available to the Company in a consulting capacity to support a smooth transition. Mike Sylvestre will assume the responsibilities of COO on an interim basis, effective today.

President and CEO, Paul Tomory, commented, As we begin the search for a permanent replacement, we are pleased to welcome Mike Sylvestre as our interim Chief Operating Officer. Mike brings decades of operational leadership and deep technical expertise, and we are confident his experience and results-driven approach will support our sites as we remain focused on execution across our operating assets and continue to develop our organic growth pipeline of projects.

Mr. Sylvestre is a seasoned mining executive with over 45 years of international experience and has held leadership roles at major, mid-tier and junior mining companies. He most recently served as Senior Vice President, Americas at Kinross Gold, retiring in 2022. Over the course of his career, he has held a range of senior operational and executive roles, with a strong track record in leading both established and start-up operations, driving safety performance, operational excellence, and sustainable practices. He currently serves on the boards of Hochschild Mining and Vista Gold Corp. Mr. Sylvestre holds a Master of Science in Mining and Mineral Engineering from McGill University and a Bachelor of Science in Mining Engineering from Queens University and is a member of the Professional Engineers of Ontario.

About Centerra Gold

Centerra Gold Inc. is a Canadian-based gold mining company focused on operating, developing, exploring and acquiring gold and copper properties in North America, Turkiye, and other markets worldwide. Centerra operates two mines: the Mount Milligan Mine in British Columbia, Canada, and the Oksut Mine in Turkiye. The Company also owns the Kemess Project in British Columbia, Canada, the Goldfield Project in Nevada, United States, and owns and operates the Molybdenum Business Unit in the United States and Canada. Centerras shares trade on the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX) under the symbol CG and on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) under the symbol CGAU. The Company is based in Toronto, Ontario, Canada.

For more information:

Lisa WilkinsonVice President, Investor Relations & Corporate Communications(416) 204-3780Lisa.Wilkinson@centerragold.com

Additional information on Centerra is available on the Companys website at www.centerragold.com, on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca and EDGAR at www.sec.gov/edgar.

Source: West Corporation

+ CARLYLE GROUP CENTERRA GOLD FILES TECHNICAL REPORT FOR THE KEMESS PROJECT

TORONTO, March 05, 2026 -- Centerra Gold Inc. (Centerra or the Company) (TSX: CG) (NYSE: CGAU) announced today that it has filed a technical report in relation to its Kemess Project, located in British Columbia, Canada.

The report was prepared in accordance with National Instrument 43-101 Standards for Disclosure for Mineral Projects and supports the disclosure outlined in Centerras news release dated January 19, 2026. The report is available on Centerras website at www.centerragold.com and on SEDAR+ atwww.sedarplus.ca.

About Centerra Gold

Centerra Gold Inc. is a Canadian-based gold mining company focused on operating, developing, exploring and acquiring gold and copper properties in North America, Turkiye, and other markets worldwide. Centerra operates two mines: the Mount Milligan Mine in British Columbia, Canada, and the Oksut Mine in Turkiye. The Company also owns the Kemess Project in British Columbia, Canada, the Goldfield Project in Nevada, United States, and owns and operates the Molybdenum Business Unit in the United States and Canada. Centerras shares trade on the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX) under the symbol CG and on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) under the symbol CGAU. The Company is based in Toronto, Ontario, Canada.

For more information:

+ CARLYLE GROUP CARLYLE TO HOST 2026 SHAREHOLDER UPDATE

WASHINGTON and NEW YORK, Feb. 02, 2026 -- Global investment firm Carlyle (NASDAQ: CG) announced today that it will host a Shareholder Update on Thursday, February 26, 2026. Carlyle employs more than 2,400 people in 27 offices across four continents. Further information is available atwww.carlyle.com. Follow Carlyle on X @OneCarlyle and LinkedIn at The Carlyle Group.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC [3 Press Releases]

+ APOLLO FUNDS ACQUIRE GATEHOUSE LIVING GROUP FROM GATEHOUSE BANK

Apollo (NYSE: APO) today announced that Apollo-managed funds have acquired Gatehouse Living Group ("GLG" or "the Group"), a vertically integrated UK residential investment and management platform, from Gatehouse Bank ("GHB"). Financial terms of the transaction were not disclosed.

The Group is comprised of its investment arm, Gatehouse Investment Management ("GIM"), as well as its property management arm, Ascend Properties ("Ascend"). Together, the Group provides institutional investors with a comprehensive suite of operational services to originate and manage new build-to-rent single family housing. As a leader in the UK, the Group has established five platforms and successfully exited three, acquiring more than 5,000 homes from the UK 's largest housebuilders. Through Ascend, the Group manages more than 10,000 homes nationwide for both itself and third-party clients, nearly 4,000 of which are operated under the resident-facing white-label Ascend Living brand.

"GLG has led the growth of institutional rental housing, creating a prominent platform that strategically supports the sector and accelerates the delivery of much-needed high-quality housing throughout the UK," said Paul Stockwell, Chief Executive Officer of GLG. "This transaction with Apollo is a testament to the strength of our holistic investment and property management platform and will support our continued expansion in the market."

"GLG, under Gatehouse Bank's stewardship, has established itself as a vertically integrated leader in the UK housing sector, supporting the supply of high-quality, professionally managed rental homes," said Edward Jones, Partner at Apollo. "We look forward to working closely with management to further enhance and expand its operational capabilities and to supporting the platform's continued success as the UK focuses on bolstering homebuilding across tenures and attracting more investment to the sector."

"We are incredibly proud of the success achieved since GLG launched its first investment in 2014 and the critical role we have played, as an early entrant, in establishing the UK Build to Rent market," said Charles Haresnape, Chief Executive Officer of GHB. "The sale is part of Gatehouse Bank's long-term retail growth strategy and will allow for further development of its home finance product offering, which has seen strong demand supporting UK resident, UK expat and international homebuyers and landlords. We are confident that GLG is well positioned today to continuing building on its strong foundation, supporting the supply of new rental homes in the UK."

GLG will continue to be led by CEO Paul Stockwell and its management team, and it will be rebranded with an announcement in due course. The Group will invest its own capital and that of third-party partners, and Ascend will maintain its property management services for external institutional portfolios.

+ APOLLO SPORTS CAPITAL COMPLETES TRANSACTION TO BECOME MAJORITY SHAREHOLDER OF ATLETICO DE MADRID

Atletico de Madrid today announced that Apollo Sports Capital ('ASC'), the global sports investment company and affiliate of Apollo (NYSE: APO), has completed its previously announced investment to become the Club's majority shareholder. As part of the transaction, the Quantum Pacific Group (QPM) will retain substantially all of its previously held stake and be the second-largest shareholder, while Miguel Angel Gil and Enrique Cerezo as well as Ares funds will remain as shareholders.

Miguel Angel Gil and Enrique Cerezo will continue to lead Atletico de Madrid as Chief Executive Officer and Chairman, respectively. As long-term investors, ASC will partner with Atletico de Madrid's management to support the Club's financial strength, sporting competitiveness and community impact. In addition, the shareholders of the Atletico de Madrid Board have approved an increase of equity and strategic capital up to an additional EUR100 million to support the Club's plans, including investment in Atletico de Madrid's teams and in major infrastructure projects as part of Ciudad del Deporte.

Chief Executive Officer of Atletico de Madrid Miguel Angel Gil, said, "We are proud to officially welcome Apollo Sports Capital to Atletico de Madrid as a committed, long-term partner and one that will build on our great legacy on and off the pitch for our fans, our players, coaches and staff, and our community. I also want to thank Wanda Group, Quantum Pacific and Ares, who have supported us through pivotal moments to make this new chapter a reality."

Apollo Partner and co-Portfolio Manager of ASC Robert Givone, said, "It is an honor for Apollo Sports Capital to become stewards of this storied franchise, partnering with Miguel Angel and the management team to back their long-term vision, investing in the club and the local community. ASC is committed to upholding the Atleti spirit and traditions in this exciting next phase."

Antoine Bonnier, CEO of Quantum Pacific (UK) LLP, said, "We are proud to be remaining as the Club's second-largest shareholder as we enter this exciting new era, and we look forward to working with Apollo, Miguel, Enrique and everyone else at Atleti to reach new heights together. Under Miguel's leadership, we have achieved a lot together on and off the pitch. Now, as we approach our second decade at Atleti we're confident that the best is yet to come."

Jim Miller, Co-Head of Ares' Sports, Media and Entertainment strategy, said, "We are excited to continue our support for Atletico de Madrid, and we are confident in the Club's future as they build on their momentum and the significant success we've achieved together."

+ APOLLO PROVIDES $1 BILLION HYBRID CAPITAL SOLUTION TO ALDAR

Apollo (NYSE: APO) today announced that Apollo-managed funds have invested $1 billion in subordinated hybrid notes issued by Aldar Properties PJSC ("Aldar"), a leading UAE based real estate developer and investment manager. The investment builds on Apollo's long-term strategic partnership with Aldar and represents Apollo's fifth investment in Aldar since 2022, bringing aggregate commitments to $2.9 billion to date.

Proceeds from the investment are intended to support Aldar's balance sheet flexibility and strength, as well as its growth agenda, which includes landbank replenishment, expansion of its develop-to-hold portfolio, and strategic acquisitions.

Apollo Partner Jamshid Ehsani said, "Completing our fifth investment with Aldar speaks directly to Apollo's ability to structure flexible capital solutions that are responsive to the needs of both our corporate clients and our investors. Since our first transaction in 2022, Aldar has gone from strength to strength, with robust performance and portfolio expansion overseen by an experienced management team. This latest investment reflects Apollo's continued commitment to Abu Dhabi and the broader region."

Faisal Falaknaz, Group Chief Financial and Sustainability Officer at Aldar, said: "This transaction highlights the strength of our long-standing partnership with Apollo and the continued confidence of major institutional investors in Aldar's strategy, financial management and growth trajectory. The issuance provides Aldar with long-term, flexible capital that enhances balance sheet resilience and supports our ability to capitalise on attractive opportunities across our core markets. Importantly, it elevates Aldar's share of stable, recurring income generated by AIP's high quality, diversified portfolio, which will continue to expand through acquisitions and our substantial develop-to-hold pipeline that is now valued at close to $5 billion."

The transaction is among the largest-ever foreign direct investments in Abu Dhabi's private sector and the largest corporate hybrid private placements in the region.

It also marks the latest transaction for Apollo's High Grade Capital Solutions business, which serves as a capital partner to many leading global companies. Apollo believes its ability to provide customized, long-dated investments is reinforced by the number of its repeat clients, having provided multiple large-scale solutions for Aldar, BP, Sony, Vonovia, Air France and the Adani-backed Mumbai Airport.

Source: Company Website

+ QATAR AIRWAYS FURTHER REBUILDS FLIGHT SCHEDULES

Qatar Airways has published its latest revised schedule, reflecting the gradual increase in flights to, and from, Doha to more than 120 destinations by mid-May 2026, across the airline's global network. All flights to, and from, Doha continue to operate through dedicated flight corridors established in close co-ordination with the Qatar Civil Aviation Authority. Please note: Flight schedules are subject to change or cancellation due to operational, regulatory, safety, or other circumstances beyond our control. To view the schedule, click here. Passengers who currently hold a confirmed booking on a flight to the destinations listed in the new schedule will be contacted with new flight information. Please be sure to check the Qatar Airways website or app and ensure your contact details are correct and updated. Source: Company Website

+ 26 MARCH 2026: QATAR AIRWAYS CONTINUES TO RESTORE FLIGHT NETWORK

Qatar Airways has published its latest revised schedule, reflecting the gradual increase in flights to and from Doha. The latest update, valid until 15 April 2026, provides additional frequencies to more than 90 destinations across Qatar Airways' global network. All flights to and from Doha continue to operate through dedicated flight corridors. These routes are established in close coordination with the Qatar Civil Aviation Authority.

Passengers who currently hold a confirmed booking on a flight to one of the destinations listed in the new schedule will be contacted with new flight information. Please be sure to check the Qatar Airways website or app and ensure your contact details are correct and updated. Passengers are kindly asked not to arrive at their departure airport unless they hold a valid, confirmed ticket for travel. Please note: Flight schedules are subject to change or cancellation due to operational, regulatory, safety, or other circumstances beyond our control.

Our teams are doing everything within the current constraints to ensure our passengers reach their destinations. We apologise for any inconvenience caused during this period of disruption and thank passengers for their patience and understanding. Source: Company Website

+ QATAR AIRWAYS TO OPERATE LIMITED RELIEF CORRIDOR FROM DOHA

Qatar Airways scheduled flight operations remain temporarily suspended due to the closure of Qatari airspace. Qatar Airways will resume operations once the Qatar Civil Aviation Authority announces the safe full reopening of Qatari airspace by the relevant authorities. A further update will be provided on 8 March by 09:00 Doha time (06:00 UTC). Following temporary authorisation from the Qatar Civil Aviation Authority confirming a safe operating corridor, Qatar Airways intends to operate repatriation flights on 07 March, departing from Hamad International Airport to the following airports: London (LHR), Paris (CDG), Madrid (MAD), Rome (FCO) and Frankfurt (FRA). Priority on these flights was given to stranded passengers with families, elderly passengers, and those with urgent medical and compassionate travel needs. Each flight was pre-allocated by Qatar Airways directly to such affected passengers. These flights do not constitute a confirmation of resumption of scheduled commercial operations. Passengers are kindly requested not to proceed to the airport unless they have already received an official notification from Qatar Airways for these flights. Qatar Airways has contacted affected passengers directly with their assigned flight details, travel arrangements and next steps. We are working around the clock to organise additional relief flights where operationally possible and will share further updates as soon as they are confirmed. Source: Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JHS-00N3-RS1N-02H9-00000-00SUNY Chancellor talks Reconnect program at GCC2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:02:38ZLexisNexisNEWSSUNY Chancellor talks Reconnect program at GCCBen Beagle

BATAVIA Sarah Reynolds of Greece is a little more than a week away from crossing the stage in Richard C. Call Arena at Genesee Community College to receive her associate degree in teacher education. She will do that having attended classes full-time while also working full-time and managing a family oh, and she's soon to get married.

Reynolds had been out of a classroom for a decade when she returned to college in 2023.

"It was scary to be my age and not knowing what to expect," Reynolds told The Daily News in an interview at the college Wednesday.

Support from family and a desire to want to do better encouraged her to take the first step. Then, the launch in fall 2025 of the SUNY Reconnect program propelled her to the finish.

Reynolds was among several students who shared their stories with SUNY Chancellor John B. King Jr. during a roundtable discussion at GCC about the SUNY Reconnect program.

SUNY Reconnect provides free community college including tuition, books, fees and supplies to New Yorkers ages 25 to 55 who do not have a college degree but are interested in pursing an associate degree in a high-need field. Those fields include advanced manufacturing, artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, engineering, technology, nursing and allied health fields, green and renewable energy and pathways to teaching in shortage areas.

More than 5,600 New York community college students are participating in the SUNY Reconnect program, including 115 at GCC. The initiative has helped boost enrollment at GCC and other community colleges.

At GCC, nursing and information technology are the areas with the most SUNY Reconnect students, according to President Craig R. Lamb.

"I think we will see continued growth in those areas," Lamb said. "Nursing is always large, and IT is seeing the biggest enrollment through SUNY Reconnect."

The respiratory care program at GCC is already seeing increased interest for the fall semester, according to Maureen Welch, the program's director.

And more programs could become eligible. Gov. Kathy Hochul's proposed budget, which remains in negotiations in Albany, has called for an expansion of SUNY Reconnect to include air traffic control and transportation, emergency management and logistics. Hochul has also called to expand nursing programs for adults who already have a previous degree, but want to go back to school to pursue nursing.

During his two-hour visit, King who also served as the U.S. Secretary of Education from 2016-17 and the state Education Commissioner from 2011-14 toured the college's respiratory care program classroom and lab and had a roundtable discussion with college officials, local and state representatives, and several SUNY Reconnect students currently enrolled at GCC.

King's visit sought to draw attention to the program, which he said serves two purposes.

"I think SUNY Reconnect really is a broader vote of confidence from the governor and the legislature in the role of community colleges in the state, both as a pipeline to transfer where folks will move on to four-year institutions to get ultimately a bachelor's degree, but really also as a pipeline to the workforce," he said.

King also wanted to hear from students both successes and the challenges they faced.

Heidi Benway-Snyder came back to school through the Reconnect program "because I wanted to be able to show my kids that even if you don't finish something, you can always finish."

She studied web design because she "was always good with computers."

"I went from being a Walmart employee for 21 years, stay-at-home mom, and my kids don't really need me anymore at the moment," Benway-Snyder said. "So I figured I'd go back and try to get my degree done."

Alexis Bruning started back at GCC in December 2024 after seeing a lot of friends go back to school. She had been an LPN for nine years "and decided that it was time to finally go back as part of GCC's program."

"At the time when I first started back, Reconnect wasn't a thing. And then as I got my studies in a little bit further it was, and since then it's paid for everything pretty much I needed except for a couple of select things," Bruning said. "All in all, I think it's a really great program."

Reynolds switched her major three times before settling into the teacher education program.

"I loved working with children," said Reynolds, who will next pursue a bachelor's degree at SUNY Brockport and hopes to work as a kindergarten or first-grade teacher. "I'm excited to see where this degree takes me."

The students praised the approachability of their professors, their willingness to accommodate students' needs and set office hours and schedules based on the Reconnect students' availability. They also liked the tutoring options.

"They really try to work with you and do the best that they can," Bruning said.

King also asked about challenges.

The rural nature of the GLOW region often presented the biggest with limited public transportation and internet connections, both students and Lamb noted. Other challenges including meeting certain program requirements such as field observations in teaching programs while scheduling around full-time jobs and help in paying for the cost of licensing exams.

Students mentioned the challenges of balancing adult responsibilities such as caring for children with their student responsibilities, but also praised the efforts of GCC to help with an on-campus child care program and other partnerships. GCC has also assisted with gas cards and mini-grants to aid with dead batteries, flat tires, or minor car repairs. In the classroom, students noted the need to stay current with technology and software.

GCC added a full-time SUNY Reconnect coordinator and part-time adult learning specialist to help the older students while also providing additional student resources such as student guides, success maps and other supports.

King has been visiting SUNY community colleges throughout the state to promote New York's free community college program for adult learners and also gather information to take back to Albany.

"Every fall I sit down with the governor with my long list of suggestions for the state of the state and the state budget and last fall I sat down and I said, 'Here are several ways we could expand the Reconnect program," King said. "She chose a few and we're doing those, but you can be confident that next fall I will be sitting down with her again with a good long list of ways that we can expand Reconnect."

---

To learn more about the SUNY Reconnect degree programs available at Genesee Community College, visit https://www.genesee.edu/freecc/ .

urn:contentItem:6JHK-FDX3-RVF9-412P-00000-00SUNY Chancellor talks Reconnect program at GCC2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:02:38ZLexisNexisNEWSSUNY Chancellor talks Reconnect program at GCCBen Beagle

BATAVIA Sarah Reynolds of Greece is a little more than a week away from crossing the stage in Richard C. Call Arena at Genesee Community College to receive her associate degree in teacher education. She will do that having attended classes full-time while also working full-time and managing a family oh, and she's soon to get married.

Reynolds had been out of a classroom for a decade when she returned to college in 2023.

"It was scary to be my age and not knowing what to expect," Reynolds told The Daily News in an interview at the college Wednesday.

Support from family and a desire to want to do better encouraged her to take the first step. Then, the launch in fall 2025 of the SUNY Reconnect program propelled her to the finish.

Reynolds was among several students who shared their stories with SUNY Chancellor John B. King Jr. during a roundtable discussion at GCC about the SUNY Reconnect program.

SUNY Reconnect provides free community college including tuition, books, fees and supplies to New Yorkers ages 25 to 55 who do not have a college degree but are interested in pursing an associate degree in a high-need field. Those fields include advanced manufacturing, artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, engineering, technology, nursing and allied health fields, green and renewable energy and pathways to teaching in shortage areas.

More than 5,600 New York community college students are participating in the SUNY Reconnect program, including 115 at GCC. The initiative has helped boost enrollment at GCC and other community colleges.

At GCC, nursing and information technology are the areas with the most SUNY Reconnect students, according to President Craig R. Lamb.

"I think we will see continued growth in those areas," Lamb said. "Nursing is always large, and IT is seeing the biggest enrollment through SUNY Reconnect."

The respiratory care program at GCC is already seeing increased interest for the fall semester, according to Maureen Welch, the program's director.

And more programs could become eligible. Gov. Kathy Hochul's proposed budget, which remains in negotiations in Albany, has called for an expansion of SUNY Reconnect to include air traffic control and transportation, emergency management and logistics. Hochul has also called to expand nursing programs for adults who already have a previous degree, but want to go back to school to pursue nursing.

During his two-hour visit, King who also served as the U.S. Secretary of Education from 2016-17 and the state Education Commissioner from 2011-14 toured the college's respiratory care program classroom and lab and had a roundtable discussion with college officials, local and state representatives, and several SUNY Reconnect students currently enrolled at GCC.

King's visit sought to draw attention to the program, which he said serves two purposes.

"I think SUNY Reconnect really is a broader vote of confidence from the governor and the legislature in the role of community colleges in the state, both as a pipeline to transfer where folks will move on to four-year institutions to get ultimately a bachelor's degree, but really also as a pipeline to the workforce," he said.

King also wanted to hear from students both successes and the challenges they faced.

Heidi Benway-Snyder came back to school through the Reconnect program "because I wanted to be able to show my kids that even if you don't finish something, you can always finish."

She studied web design because she "was always good with computers."

"I went from being a Walmart employee for 21 years, stay-at-home mom, and my kids don't really need me anymore at the moment," Benway-Snyder said. "So I figured I'd go back and try to get my degree done."

Alexis Bruning started back at GCC in December 2024 after seeing a lot of friends go back to school. She had been an LPN for nine years "and decided that it was time to finally go back as part of GCC's program."

"At the time when I first started back, Reconnect wasn't a thing. And then as I got my studies in a little bit further it was, and since then it's paid for everything pretty much I needed except for a couple of select things," Bruning said. "All in all, I think it's a really great program."

Reynolds switched her major three times before settling into the teacher education program.

"I loved working with children," said Reynolds, who will next pursue a bachelor's degree at SUNY Brockport and hopes to work as a kindergarten or first-grade teacher. "I'm excited to see where this degree takes me."

The students praised the approachability of their professors, their willingness to accommodate students' needs and set office hours and schedules based on the Reconnect students' availability. They also liked the tutoring options.

"They really try to work with you and do the best that they can," Bruning said.

King also asked about challenges.

The rural nature of the GLOW region often presented the biggest with limited public transportation and internet connections, both students and Lamb noted. Other challenges including meeting certain program requirements such as field observations in teaching programs while scheduling around full-time jobs and help in paying for the cost of licensing exams.

Students mentioned the challenges of balancing adult responsibilities such as caring for children with their student responsibilities, but also praised the efforts of GCC to help with an on-campus child care program and other partnerships. GCC has also assisted with gas cards and mini-grants to aid with dead batteries, flat tires, or minor car repairs. In the classroom, students noted the need to stay current with technology and software.

GCC added a full-time SUNY Reconnect coordinator and part-time adult learning specialist to help the older students while also providing additional student resources such as student guides, success maps and other supports.

King has been visiting SUNY community colleges throughout the state to promote New York's free community college program for adult learners and also gather information to take back to Albany.

"Every fall I sit down with the governor with my long list of suggestions for the state of the state and the state budget and last fall I sat down and I said, 'Here are several ways we could expand the Reconnect program," King said. "She chose a few and we're doing those, but you can be confident that next fall I will be sitting down with her again with a good long list of ways that we can expand Reconnect."

---

To learn more about the SUNY Reconnect degree programs available at Genesee Community College, visit https://www.genesee.edu/freecc/ .

Fri May 1, 2026: National Grid Expands DLR Technology Across 585km of UK Transmission Routes

National Grid PLC is set to expand its Dynamic Line Rating (DLR) technology to 585 kilometers of existing transmission routes across the UK. This initiative, announced on the 1st of May 2026, aims to deliver substantial savings for consumers, projected at up to £50 million in constraint costs over the next five years.

The DLR system utilizes advanced sensors to continuously monitor electricity transmission lines, calculating a dynamic line rating based on various factors, including wire specifications and weather conditions. This approach provides a more accurate assessment of the maximum power carrying capacity of transmission lines compared to traditional assumptions, which often underestimate their capabilities.

National Grid estimates that the implementation of DLR technology enables transmission lines to handle an average of 8% more electricity safely. The technology was initially tested in New York and Massachusetts in partnership with US-based provider LineVision before being introduced to the UK through a trial program in Cumbria that began in 2022. Following the success of the pilot, National Grid expanded the DLR system last year, adding an additional 275 kilometers of transmission routes.

The upcoming rollout will be conducted in partnership with LineVision, Belgian grid monitoring firm Ampacimon, and Danish cybersecurity company Heimdall. National Grid anticipates that the majority of the upgrades will be completed by 2028, with plans to incorporate drone technology for sensor installation on live power lines. Alice Delahunty, president of National Grid Electricity Transmission, emphasized that these innovations will not only enhance grid capacity but also facilitate the integration of renewable energy into the system.

The expansion of the grid's operational infrastructure is especially critical given the increasing challenges of grid capacity in the UK. Earlier this year, Foresight Solar Fund's investment manager, Toby Virno, highlighted the alarming rise in curtailment costs, which have reached £2.3 billion as the UK continues to commission more renewable energy capacity than its current grid can effectively accommodate. This DLR expansion by National Grid could be a pivotal move in addressing this pressing issue.

Thu Apr 30, 2026: National Grid PLC Faces Dual Challenge of AI and Decarbonisation

The intersection of artificial intelligence (AI) and the decarbonisation of electricity generation is positioning National Grid PLC at a critical juncture. With a pressing need to achieve Net Zero emissions, the UK's power sector faces a transformative period defined by increased reliance on renewable energy sources and advancements in AI technology. The date, 30th April 2026, marks a significant moment as the National Grid grapples with the simultaneous pressures of rising electricity demand and the expanding footprint of AI-driven data centres.

The implications of electrification for transport, heating, and industrial processes suggest that Britain's electricity requirements could soar by as much as 15% by 2030, and a staggering 40% by 2035, according to the National Energy System Operator (NESO). Contributing to this challenge is the anticipated surge in electricity demand from data centres, which are expected to double globally by 2030, with Europe alone projected to experience a 70% increase in demand. This concentrated demand poses unique challenges for National Grid PLC, particularly in terms of reliability and grid management.

The UK is currently witnessing a significant build-out of data centre infrastructure, with the government estimating the co-location market at approximately 1.6GW of IT load. Major developments are underway, including a 1GW data centre campus in North Lincolnshire and a 720MW facility in Northumberland. While the current contribution of data centres to the UK's overall electricity demand is relatively modest, projections indicate that their consumption could rise to 18TWh by 2031, representing nearly 6% of total demand. This shift underscores the urgency for National Grid PLC to adapt its infrastructure to accommodate these new loads.

Despite the challenges presented by AI and increasing energy demands, there is also a silver lining. AI technologies could provide crucial solutions for utilities and energy-tech companies, enabling them to optimise energy consumption, enhance customer engagement, and foster a more resilient electricity system. Platforms like Kraken are leading the way by not only managing billing but also facilitating flexible energy solutions, such as optimising electric vehicle charging. The ability of AI to improve forecasting, demand visibility, and automated flexibility is essential for the modernisation of National Grid PLC's operations.

In conclusion, while AI-driven data centres will undeniably exert additional pressure on the National Grid, they also represent a pathway to a more dynamic, responsive, and intelligent power system. If harnessed effectively, AI can empower National Grid PLC to streamline operations, reduce waste, and unlock new value-added services that benefit both consumers and the environment. As the UK navigates the dual challenges of the Net Zero transition and the burgeoning AI landscape, the need for innovative, AI-powered solutions has never been more critical.
