urn:contentItem:6JHK-KSM3-S0RF-V52G-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - THE RECENT SECTOR HEADLINES WITH PEERS2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:02:38ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - THE RECENT SECTOR HEADLINES WITH PEERS

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [1 News That Matters]

+ MORGAN LEWIS STRENGTHENS EMPLOYEE BENEFITS TEAM WITH PILLSBURY TALENT

Morgan Lewis & Bockius LLP has announced the addition of a prominent attorney from Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP to its Employee Benefits team. This strategic move is seen as a response to the growing complexities in employee benefits law and the increasing demand for expert legal guidance in this area. The attorney, whose experience spans multiple facets of employee benefits, is expected to enhance Morgan Lewis’ capabilities in providing comprehensive legal services to its clients.The announcement comes as Morgan Lewis looks to bolster its offerings in employee benefits law, particularly in the wake of several recent regulatory changes. The attorney's arrival on 08th April 2026 is intended to strengthen the firm's position in an increasingly competitive legal landscape. Morgan Lewis has consistently focused on expanding its practice areas to meet client needs, and this latest addition is part of that ongoing strategy.Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman, known for its robust practice in employee benefits, has seen a number of its attorneys transition to other firms in recent years. The departing attorney brings a wealth of experience in compliance and regulatory issues, which will be invaluable to Morgan Lewis as it navigates the complexities of employee benefit plans and pension laws. This move not only reflects the attorney's individual career advancement but also highlights the fluid nature of talent within the legal sector.Morgan Lewis has a reputation for its deep expertise in various practice areas, and the inclusion of the Pillsbury attorney is expected to further enhance its service offerings. The firm has been proactive in adapting to changes in legislation affecting employee benefits, ensuring that its clients remain compliant and well-informed. By integrating new talent, Morgan Lewis aims to provide even more specialized services tailored to the evolving needs of its diverse client base.As the legal field continues to evolve, the strategic hiring of top talent from firms like Pillsbury is critical for maintaining a competitive edge. Morgan Lewis’ latest hiring signals its commitment to delivering high-quality legal solutions in employee benefits, reinforcing its reputation as a leading firm in this practice area. The legal community will be watching closely to see how this new addition impacts Morgan Lewis’ capabilities and client service in the months ahead.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 News That Matters]

+ ASHURST PERKINS COIE MERGER GAINS STRONG SUPPORT FROM PARTNERS

The merger of global law firms Ashurst and Perkins Coie into the entity Ashurst Perkins Coie has received overwhelming support from the partnerships of both firms. This decision was finalized following a consultation period, with the approval announced on 13th April 2026. The merger is expected to close in the third quarter of the year, contingent upon customary closing conditions.Mark Birnbaum, executive committee chair of Perkins Coie, emphasized the significance of this approval, stating that it reflects the partners' commitment to creating a forward-looking firm. He noted that the merger underlines a shared confidence in their capacity to grow and deliver exceptional legal services in an evolving global market. Paul Jenkins, CEO of Ashurst, also highlighted the alignment between the two firms, asserting that their combined strengths will benefit their clients significantly.The merged firm aims to focus on key sectors including technology, energy, infrastructure, and financial services. Bill Malley, firmwide managing partner at Perkins Coie, expressed optimism that the combination would enhance the firms' abilities to meet clients’ complex cross-border legal needs. The new entity is set to boast approximately 3,000 lawyers across 52 offices in 32 countries, with major hubs located in Seattle, London, Sydney, and New York.The merger, first announced last year, brings together two firms with combined revenues of around USD 2.8 billion. Their lawyers have extensive experience in international markets, having worked in cities such as Brussels, Dubai, Frankfurt, Hong Kong, Paris, Seoul, Shenzhen, Singapore, and Tokyo. The leadership of the new firm will be shared by Malley and Jenkins as global co-CEOs, while Ashurst's global chair, Karen Davies, and Perkins Coie's corporate practice chair, Brian Eiting, will serve as co-chairs.As the legal landscape continues to evolve, the Ashurst Perkins Coie combination is poised to enhance its competitive edge by leveraging the strengths of both firms. This strategic move not only positions the new entity to better serve its clients but also sets the stage for sustained growth and performance in the legal industry.

+ PERKINS COIE AND ASHURST UNITE TO FORM ASHURST PERKINS COIE

NEW YORK — Perkins Coie and Ashurst announced today that the partnerships of both firms have voted overwhelmingly to approve their proposed combination. The approval follows consultation from both firms and reflects strong support for the combination of two law firms with highly complementary geographic reach, sector depth, and practice strength. The date of this announcement is recorded as 2026-04-14 16:18:02.950901.Upon closing, Ashurst Perkins Coie will emerge as a globally integrated firm with combined revenues of approximately US$2.8 billion. The new entity will boast around 3,000 lawyers across more than 50 offices, including flagship hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney, and New York. Ashurst Perkins Coie will be particularly noted for its strengths in the technology, energy and infrastructure, and financial services sectors—industries that are pivotal to the ongoing global economic transformation.Bill Malley, the Firmwide Managing Partner at Perkins Coie, remarked on the significance of this merger by stating, 'Our partnership's approval reflects a shared belief that combining to form Ashurst Perkins Coie will create a truly differentiated global legal platform—one with the scale, sector depth, and technological leadership to meet our clients' increasingly complex, cross-border needs.' This sentiment underscores the strategic vision behind the combination.Paul Jenkins, Global CEO of Ashurst, echoed Malley’s sentiments, emphasizing the strong alignment between the two firms. 'This vote confirms the strong alignment between our firms and our joint ambition for the future,' he stated, highlighting the complementary expertise across sectors and practice areas that will benefit clients. Jenkins added that the merger will create a compelling platform for top legal talent.The combination remains subject to customary closing conditions and is expected to finalize in the third quarter of 2026. As the legal landscape becomes more complex, Ashurst Perkins Coie aims to position itself as a trusted advisor, ready to navigate the intricacies of the global market and deliver exceptional legal services.

+ PERKINS COIE REENTERS CHINA MARKET WITH NEW SHENZHEN OFFICE

Perkins Coie has officially opened a representative office in Shenzhen, marking the firm's significant comeback in the Chinese market. This development comes on the heels of a strategic decision to re-establish an on-the-ground presence in the region, with the office inaugurated on 14th April 2026.The Seattle-based law firm had previously operated offices in Beijing and Shanghai, which were closed in 2024. At that time, Perkins Coie indicated plans to pivot towards Shenzhen, citing the city’s rapid growth as a key tech and manufacturing hub. The firm aims to leverage this new office to enhance its support for Asia-based companies navigating US and cross-border legal needs.Leading the Shenzhen office will be Bing Ai, serving as chief representative and managing partner, along with Wei Yuan, who will act as a representative and litigation partner. Both lawyers are US-trained and bring extensive experience in advising technology-focused clients. Ai specializes in patent procurement and portfolio management in sectors such as semiconductors and medical devices, while Yuan is adept at handling patent infringement disputes.Bill Malley, Perkins Coie’s managing partner, emphasized the firm's commitment to providing US legal expertise across borders. He noted that the opening of the Shenzhen office reflects a dedication to assisting clients in navigating the complexities of global markets, especially in light of the increasing challenges they face.As Perkins Coie re-establishes its presence in mainland China, it stands apart from the trend of office closures and headcount reductions by many leading US firms due to geopolitical tensions and regulatory challenges. However, several international firms have recently opened offices in Shenzhen, attracted by its status within the Greater Bay Area, which has seen substantial economic growth. The firm, known for its expertise in private equity and technology law, is investing in artificial intelligence tools to enhance its legal services and optimize operational efficiency.On 10th April 2026, Goodwin Procter announced its strategic decision to integrate AI technologies into its practice, a move that positions the firm among industry leaders adapting to technological advancements. This shift is part of a broader trend where law firms are increasingly leveraging AI to streamline processes and improve client service.The integration of AI tools is expected to transform various aspects of Goodwin Procter's operations, from research and document review to predictive analytics in case management. By embracing AI, the firm aims to reduce time spent on routine tasks and enable its attorneys to focus on more complex legal issues that require human expertise.Goodwin Procter's commitment to adopting AI technologies underscores a significant shift in the legal industry, where firms are recognizing the value of innovation in maintaining a competitive edge. As clients demand faster and more efficient services, the move towards AI-native practices is becoming essential for law firms looking to thrive in the evolving market.The firm's foray into AI is indicative of a larger movement among its peers, as other major legal players also adopt similar strategies. With this trend gaining momentum, Goodwin Procter is poised to not only enhance its service offerings but also set new standards in the legal profession.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ ORRICK WELCOMES SENIOR IP ATTORNEY FROM USPTO

Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe has strengthened its intellectual property (IP) practice by hiring a senior attorney from the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO). This strategic addition is expected to enhance Orrick's capabilities in navigating complex patent issues, particularly as the firm continues to expand its footprint in the technology and telecommunications sectors.The new hire will officially join the firm on the 28th of April, 2026, bringing with them a wealth of experience from their tenure at the USPTO. With an extensive background in patent examination and litigation, the attorney's expertise aligns well with Orrick's focus on providing comprehensive legal solutions to its clients in rapidly evolving industries.Orrick's managing partner commented on the significance of this hire, noting that the firm's commitment to offering top-tier IP services is underscored by the addition of professionals who possess a deep understanding of the intricacies of patent law. This move is seen as part of Orrick's broader strategy to leverage the changing landscape of intellectual property rights in the digital age.The firm's IP practice has been gaining momentum in recent years, particularly in light of increasing demand for legal counsel in technology-driven sectors. By enhancing its team with experienced attorneys from key regulatory bodies like the USPTO, Orrick is well-positioned to meet the needs of clients facing complex challenges in patent law.As Orrick continues to attract talent from leading institutions, the legal community is keenly observing how these strategic hires will influence the firm's growth trajectory and its ability to provide innovative legal solutions. Investors and clients alike are likely to be interested in the potential impact of this development on Orrick's competitive standing in the market.

+ ORRICK WELCOMES YOUTH ONLINE SAFETY EXPERT NICOLE LOPEZ

Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe has announced the addition of Nicole Lopez, a leading authority in youth online safety policy and litigation, to its team. This strategic move comes at a crucial time as the firm aims to enhance its capabilities in navigating the evolving landscape of online safety. On 28th April 2026, Lopez expressed her enthusiasm for the new role and the opportunity to make a broader impact in the field.Lopez, who previously held a significant position at Meta, emphasized the importance of collaboration in her new role at Orrick. "I am so immensely proud of the pioneering work we did at Meta as a team, where I worked with incredibly talented colleagues who cared so deeply about getting it right," she stated. Her experience and expertise will be invaluable as Orrick seeks to provide comprehensive solutions for its clients facing increasing scrutiny over online safety issues.The urgency of youth online safety has escalated, with 25 state legislatures actively considering various forms of legislation aimed at safeguarding minors in digital environments. Jeremy Kudon, who leads Orrick’s State Legislative practice, noted, "We are facing a watershed moment. Online safety is top of mind for innovators, government, teens and parents, and society at large." Lopez's leadership in this conversation positions Orrick to effectively guide clients through the legislative maze.Aravind Swaminathan, Orrick’s Tech Sector Co-Leader, highlighted Lopez's multifaceted expertise, stating, "Nicole is part policy expert, part litigation and regulatory strategist, and part business advisor – she will be an extraordinary resource to our clients." As artificial intelligence continues to reshape the digital landscape, the need for proactive measures in youth safety is more pressing than ever, and Orrick is poised to be at the forefront of these changes.Orrick's commitment to developing robust online safety solutions is further exemplified by its Online Safety Law Center, which aims to keep clients informed of rapid developments in this area. With Nicole Lopez joining the team, the firm is well-positioned to advocate for effective policies and practices that balance innovation and child protection, ensuring that both companies and young users can thrive in a safer online environment.

+ ORRICK WELCOMES FORMER META YOUTH SAFETY CHIEF TO ITS TEAM

Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe has fortified its legal team with the appointment of the former head of youth safety policy at Meta, bringing notable expertise to the firm. This strategic hire aligns with Orrick's ongoing commitment to enhance its capabilities in digital security and compliance as the landscape of technology law continues to evolve.On the 23rd of April 2026, the firm announced this significant addition, underscoring its focus on safeguarding clients against emerging risks in the digital space. The former Meta executive, who has a robust background in policy formulation and implementation, is expected to contribute valuable insights into privacy and regulatory matters, particularly relating to the protection of young users in digital environments.The hiring of such a high-profile figure from Meta reflects Orrick's proactive approach in adapting to the shifting legal requirements surrounding youth safety online. As technology companies face increasing scrutiny over their practices, Orrick is positioning itself as a leader in providing comprehensive legal support tailored to the complexities of digital safety and compliance.Orrick's decision to engage with industry veterans like the former Meta youth safety policy head highlights the firm's strategy to not only keep pace with regulatory developments but also to anticipate the needs of its clients in a rapidly changing legal landscape. This move is likely to resonate well with clients who are navigating the multifaceted challenges of technology-related legal issues.As Orrick continues to expand its footprint in this crucial area of law, stakeholders are keenly observing how the integration of expertise from Meta will enhance the firm's offerings. The legal community is eager to see the impact of this new appointment on Orrick's ability to advise clients effectively on youth safety and digital compliance matters.

value.Title: Convalt agrees to pay $1 million loan back to industrial development agency
value.Overview: Watertown Daily Times (New York), STATE AND REGIONAL NEWS, 552words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJ0-WT73-RT5J-Y2T3-00000-00Convalt agrees to pay $1 million loan back to industrial development agency2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:02:37ZLexisNexisSTATE AND REGIONAL NEWSConvalt agrees to pay $1 million loan back to industrial development agencyCraig FoxWatertown Daily Times, N.Y.

WATERTOWN — The company that had planned to build a giant solar panel manufacturing plant on a site near the Watertown International Airport is going to pay back every penny from a $1.05 million loan that it had with the Jefferson County Industrial Development Agency.

Convalt Energy defaulted on the loan with the JCIDA after failing to build the 300,000-square-foot plant on the Route 12F site in the town of Hounsfield. The JCIDA subsequently sued the company in state Supreme Court.

After months of negotiations, the JCIDA’s board on Thursday morning approved the “settlement agreement” that calls for paying off the Convalt loan in its entirety.

The company has already put an undisclosed amount of money in an escrow account and will make the second payment in November to pay off the rest of the loan, JCIDA board chairman Rob Aliasso said before the vote. Board member Edward Waldroff abstained because he became friends with Convalt President Hari Achuthan.

The amount of the two payments and how much the company will pay in legal bills were not made public on Thursday as the JCIDA waits for a legal document to be forwarded to the agency, said Marshall Weir, CEO of the Jefferson County Local Development Corp.

“It’s everything we needed to complete the matter, but it was not as quick as would have liked,” Aliasso said

The JCIDA had two attorneys working on the settlement agreement for months. Convalt’s attorney Ryan T. Donovan could not be reached for comment.

Despite the lawsuit against the company and its response in court papers, the settlement ended up being worked out amicably, Weir said.

In November 2024, the JCIDA and Convalt parted ways after the company failed to fulfill its obligations on a land development agreement to build on the 88-acre site.

The Hounsfield project was once billed as the largest economic development project in Jefferson County’s history.

Plans were announced in February 2021 for Convalt, then a New York City-based renewable energy company, to invest $650 million in the project that would manufacture solar panels, initially creating hundreds of jobs, but projected to employ thousands over time. A sister company, DigiCollect, would also build a facility in the airport industrial park.

Achuthan blamed a glut of solar products coming into the U.S. from China and deep cuts in product prices, harming the feasibility of the large project. He has also indicated that it was taking more time to obtain financing for the project. He also blamed the COVID pandemic for delaying its start.

Convalt still owns a nearly 75,000-square-foot building at 511 Pearl St. where it stored solar panel manufacturing equipment that was trucked from a defunct plant in Oregon and was going to be used in its venture here.

Three Toyota Sequoia SUVs also were left behind in the Pearl Street property’s parking lot. The company had a fleet of the SUVs in Watertown when it had a few dozen employees working in Watertown.

While the Convalt project never went forward, the 88-acre site is shovel-ready with a large pad to build on and sewer and water already available. Weir said the JCIDA “has a couple of leads” interested in the site, but a deal is not imminent.

value.Title: Studies from Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR) Further Understanding of Technology (An Update On the Use of Forward Osmosis Desalination Technology for Water Treatment: Current Trends and Future Research Outlooks)

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHM-T483-S7V4-62KR-00000-00Studies from Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR) Further Understanding of Technology (An Update On the Use of Forward Osmosis Desalination Technology for Water Treatment: Current Trends and Future Research Outlooks)2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:02:37ZLexisNexisTECHNOLOGYStudies from Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR) Further Understanding of Technology (An Update On the Use of Forward Osmosis Desalination Technology for Water Treatment: Current Trends and Future Research Outlooks)


2026 MAY 07 (NewsRx) -- By a News Reporter-Staff News Editor at Tech Daily News -- Current study results on Technology have been published. According to news reporting from Pretoria, South Africa, by NewsRx journalists, research stated, "The prevailing water security plights grossly hinged on climate change and population growth, leading to water scarcity, unequal access, and pollution that perpetually exacerbate the ever-increasing crisis and availability of water in the world, continue to call for prudent and pragmatic ways to manage this circular resource (i.e., water). Therefore, the adoption of economical and eco-friendly water treatment technology in response to the need for clean water, along with measures to mitigate energy constraints attached to energy-intensive and hefty water treatment facilities, becomes a panacea."


The news correspondents obtained a quote from the research from the Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR), "Hence, this state-of-the-art review highlights milestones made to fasttrack commercial adoption of forward osmosis (FO) technology for desalinating wastewater, brackish and seawater in a quest to grossly increase clean water availability. Herein, FO membrane technology was distilled as a suitable candidate to offer enormous opportunities for the rapidly expanding and emerging desalination industry, which is presently dominated by reverse osmosis (RO) and thermal technologies. An analysis of FO commercialisation showed that North America (40%), Europe (30%) and Asia-Pacific (25%) are leading regions in the water sector. This also corroborated the market analysis results, which estimated a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 10.5% as the market is forecasted to reach 1.774 billion $USD in 2031, showing a substantial increase from 975 million $USD, which was reported for 2025. Moreover, misconceptions and inconsistencies associated with FO were also reviewed in order to update the knowledge base, along with the drawbacks and their respective mitigation strategies. Furthermore, high flux regenerative draw solutes, hybrid renewable energy powered FO systems, minimal liquid discharge (MLD) and/or zero liquid discharge (ZLD) and systems with less concentration polarization (CP), reverse solute diffusion (RSD), and membrane fouling are critical for achieving sustainability and efficiency in FO desalination processes, and this has been meticulously underscored in this much-needed review paper."
According to the news reporters, the research concluded: "Future research outlook, critical gaps and avenues of consideration are also elucidated."
This research has been peer-reviewed.
For more information on this research see: An Update On the Use of Forward Osmosis Desalination Technology for Water Treatment: Current Trends and Future Research Outlooks. Desalination and Water Treatment, 2026;326. Desalination and Water Treatment can be contacted at: Elsevier Science Inc, Ste 800, 230 Park Ave, New York, NY 10169, USA.
Our news journalists report that additional information may be obtained by contacting Vhahangwele Masindi, Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR), Water Research Center, Smart Pl Cluster, Pob 395, Meiring Naude Rd, Za-0184 Pretoria, South Africa. Additional authors for this research include Matsie Malatji, Tebogo P. Mashifana, Siwela J. Baloyi and Khathutshelo L. Muedi.
The direct object identifier (DOI) for that additional information is: https://doi.org/10.1016/j.dwt.2026.101744. This DOI is a link to an online electronic document that is either free or for purchase, and can be your direct source for a journal article and its citation.
Keywords for this news article include: Pretoria, South Africa, Africa, Technology, Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR).

value.Title: Primoris Services Corporation Shareholders Are Encouraged to Reach Out to Johnson Fistel for More Information About Potentially Recovering Their Losses

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHK-KX23-S7FJ-14DY-00000-00Primoris Services Corporation Shareholders Are Encouraged to Reach Out to Johnson Fistel for More Information About Potentially Recovering Their Losses2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:02:37ZLexisNexisPrimoris Services Corporation Shareholders Are Encouraged to Reach Out to Johnson Fistel for More Information About Potentially Recovering Their Losses

(GlobeNewswire) - Johnson Fistel, PLLP is investigating potential claims on behalf of investors of Primoris Services Corporation (NYSE: PRIM). The investigation focuses on Primoriss executive officers and whether investor losses may be recovered under federal securities laws.

What if I purchased Primoris securities?

If you purchased Primoris securities and suffered losses on your investment, join our investigation now: Click here to join the investigation.

Or for more information, contact Jim Baker at jimb@johnsonfistel.com or (619) 814-4471.

There is no cost or obligation to you.

Background of the investigation

On May 5, 2026, Primoris reported its first quarter 2026 financial results and updated its full-year outlook. Among other things, the Company disclosed revenue of $1.6 billion, down 5.4% compared to the prior-year period, and net income of $17.4 million, compared to $44.2 million in the prior-year period.

Primoris also disclosed adjusted EBITDA of $60.5 million, down 39.1% compared to the first quarter of 2025, and adjusted EPS of $0.59, compared to $0.98 in the prior-year period.

In addition, Primoris disclosed that revenue in its Energy segment decreased by $152.9 million, or 13.8%, compared to the prior-year period. The Company attributed the decrease primarily to lower renewable energy activity due to slower-than-anticipated starts of new projects, release of new work, and financial close associated with certain projects.

Primoris further disclosed that Energy segment operating income decreased by $49.1 million, or 62.2%, compared to the prior-year period, due to decreased revenue and increased costs on certain renewable energy projects. The Company stated that these higher costs were driven in part by project redesign efforts, changes in project sequencing, labor productivity challenges, and unfavorable weather conditions. Energy gross profit as a percentage of revenue declined to 7.6%, compared to 10.7% in the prior-year period.

Primoris also reduced its full-year 2026 outlook, including adjusted EPS guidance of $4.80 to $5.00, compared to prior guidance of $5.80 to $6.00, and adjusted EBITDA guidance of $480 million to $500 million, compared to prior guidance of $560 million to $580 million.

Following this disclosure, Primoriss stock price declined sharply, damaging investors.

In light of this disclosure, Johnson Fistel is investigating whether Primoris complied with the federal securities laws. If you suffered losses from your investment in Primoris stock, contact Johnson Fistel.

About Johnson Fistel, PLLP | Securities Fraud & Investor Rights

Johnson Fistel, PLLP is a nationally recognized shareholder-rights law firm with offices in California, New York, Georgia, Idaho, and Colorado. The firm represents individual and institutional investors in shareholder derivative and securities class action lawsuits and also assists foreign investors who purchased shares on U.S. exchanges. To learn more, visit www.johnsonfistel.com.

Achievements

In 2024, Johnson Fistel was ranked among the Top 10 Plaintiff Law Firms by ISS Securities Class Action Services, reflecting the firms effectiveness in advocating for investors and recovering approximately $90,725,000 for clients in cases where it served as lead or co-lead counsel.

Attorney advertising.

Past results do not guarantee future outcomes.

Services may be performed by attorneys in any of our offices.

Johnson Fistel, PLLP has paid for the dissemination of this promotional communication, and Frank J. Johnson is the attorney responsible for its content.

value.Title: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - SECTOR INSIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHD-FJP3-S0BP-Y0B7-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - SECTOR INSIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:32ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - SECTOR INSIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES SECOND CLASS OF U.S. BIG BETS FELLOWS AT BALTIMORE CONVENING

10 bold innovators from across the United States named to The Rockefeller Foundation fellowship dedicated to driving economic opportunity and helping their communities thrive.

Fellows focusing on distinct projects to help their communities realize economic opportunities, including: expanding workforce pathways, deploying blended capital for climate resilience, building AI-powered talent-matching tools, scaling registered apprenticeships, and strengthening regional innovation ecosystems.

Source: Company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ACCELERATES U.S. ECONOMIC SOLUTIONS AT "BIG BETS FOR AMERICA: BALTIMORE"

BALTIMORE | April 21, 2026 - The Rockefeller Foundation today hosted Big Bets for America: Baltimore, bringing together more than 250 leaders spanning policy, philanthropic, private, and non-profit sectors in Baltimore to surface, accelerate, and scale ambitious solutions to the country's most pressing challenges. With a Steering Committee - consisting of Abell Foundation, Baltimore Community Foundation, Baltimore Homecoming, Greater Baltimore Committee, and The Harry and Jeanette Weinberg Foundation - and strategic partners Greater Washington Partnership, Baltimore Development Corporation, and Johns Hopkins University, the Foundation and event participants, including Maryland Governor Wes Moore, Baltimore Mayor Brandon Scott, and Cleveland Mayor Justin Bibb, announced new initiatives and innovative collaborations to create economic pathways and advance opportunities for communities across the State of Maryland and nationwide. In Baltimore, The Rockefeller Foundation also launched a $100 million commitment to connect America's workers to good jobs and its next class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows.

Source: Company Website

+ STATEMENT FROM DR. RAJIV J. SHAH, PRESIDENT OF THE ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION, ON CHANGE TO U.S. AID APPROACH

For decades, American global leadership has been built on a foundational, bipartisan belief that helping those who need it most is also an investment in our own security and prosperity. A shift away from development assistance to focus solely on trade raises serious concerns about the implications for the world's most vulnerable people and for long‑term U.S. interests.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ FORD FOUNDATION PROMOTE THE VOTE FUND: ENSURING EVERY VOICE IS HEARD IN MICHIGAN

In Michigan, local leaders work tirelessly to ensure that voting is accessible, elections are free and fair, and every eligible voter knows their rights. This strong civic participation doesn't happen overnight: It's the result of year-round, coordinated work by organizations to help people understand their rights, navigate the voting process, and come together in their communities to engage and collaborate on meaningful changes.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION THE COST OF I DO IN THE DISABILITY COMMUNITY

When Patrice Jetter and Garry Wickham became engaged, the first decisions were of small, giddy consequence: where to hold the ceremony, who to invite, what to wear. But soon enough, a far weightier question eclipsed them all: whether they could afford to marry at all. They learned that, as people with disabilities who receive Supplemental Security Income (SSI) and Medicaid coverage from the American government, their benefits will likely be cut if they wed, or even moved in together.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES 2026 COHORT OF FORD GLOBAL FELLOWSHIP

NEW YORK - The Ford Foundation today announced the 2026 cohort of the Ford Global Fellowship, welcoming 32 leaders into a burgeoning network that now exceeds 150 fellows working across Africa, Asia, the Americas, and the Middle East. This year's cohort reinforces Ford's 90-year commitment to strengthening democratic values by investing in those closest to the world's most persistent inequalities.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: Skyview Ventures, HiveTracks expand partnership to multi-state solar portfolio

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHD-95G3-SFDD-V19G-00000-00Skyview Ventures, HiveTracks expand partnership to multi-state solar portfolio2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:02:37ZLexisNexisSkyview Ventures, HiveTracks expand partnership to multi-state solar portfolio

Renewable energy investor Skyview Ventures and its solar firm Davis Hill Development has expanded its partnership with HiveTracks to integrate apiaries and environmental monitoring across its solar portfolio.

The companys current solar offerings span multiple states, including Maryland, Connecticut, and New York, plus Washington, D.C. Using HiveTracks biodiversity intelligence platform will allow the company to scale data-driven environmental monitoring and managed pollinator programs across several solar projects, incorporating pollinator habitats into existing solar projects.

This partnership reflects our broader vision that renewable energy projects should be developed with long-term environmental stewardship in mind, says Andy Karetsky, CEO of Skyview Ventures. By integrating biodiversity monitoring, pollinator habitats, and data-driven land management into our solar portfolio, we are demonstrating that solar projects can deliver environmental benefits beyond clean energy generation and play a meaningful role in supporting local ecosystems.

Skyview and HiveTracks have been working together since 2025, the companies say, beginning their collaboration with eight solar projects across New York, West Virginia, Tennessee, and Connecticut. Thanks to the partnerships initial success, the two firms have planned expansion to additional Davis Hill sites throughout 2026.

Local beekeepers will tend to each solar site throughout the lifespan of its given apiary, the company says. Beyond the production of bottles of honey which in 2025 was given away to food banks the apiaries will serve as environmental indicators, giving the companies numbers on ecosystem health and land use impact over time.

solar apiary

Apiary analysis

As part of the agreement, HiveTrack will collect environmental metrics for the solar sites, both on a per-site and portfolio-wide level, company officials say. The metrics will give insight to pollinator and plant diversity and abundance, as well as testing for eDNA and ecotoxicology in each hive.

Solar energy already provides environmental benefits, but with HiveTracks, we saw an opportunity to go further, says Maria Morales Ferrebus, solar development associate at Skyview Ventures. In our first year, the data provided valuable insights into ecosystem health across our sites. For example, we identified areas with low plant diversity, which can reduce resilience to drought conditions and increase erosion risk.

By implementing HiveTracks recommendations, we can adapt our operations and maintenance practices to enhance plant diversity, improve soil health, and strengthen pollinator habitats.

The apiary program currently spans multiple projects throughout varying stages of development, the company says, with some affected projects already fully operational. This approach allows Skyview to monitor environmental impacts of solar projects through each stage of development, representatives say, with HiveTracks providing site-specific recommendations.

The heart of the program reflects a wider trend around the solar industry, Skyview says, where developers now have an outsized focus on multi-use land strategies. As part of the shift, solar projects have become synonymous with farming, habitat restoration, and biodiversity initiatives around the U.S. and beyond.

Solar sites represent a unique opportunity to support pollinators and monitor biodiversity at scale, says Max Rnzel, CEO of HiveTracks. Our partnership with Skyview Ventures demonstrates how beekeeping, environmental data, and renewable energy infrastructure can work together to create measurable environmental impact.

value.Title: Shareholders Are Encouraged to Reach Out to Johnson Fistel for More Information About Potentially Recovering Their Losses

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHD-NKV3-S1G2-W2B5-00000-00Shareholders Are Encouraged to Reach Out to Johnson Fistel for More Information About Potentially Recovering Their Losses2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:02:37ZLexisNexisShareholders Are Encouraged to Reach Out to Johnson Fistel for More Information About Potentially Recovering Their Losses

PRIMORIS SERVICES CORP ("PRIM-Q") - Shareholders Are Encouraged to Reach Out to Johnson Fistel - for More Information About Potentially Recovering Their Losses Johnson Fistel, PLLP is investigating potential claims on behalf of investors of Primoris Services Corporation. The investigation focuses on Primoriss executive officers and whether investor losses may be recovered under federal securities laws. What if I purchased Primoris securities? If you purchased Primoris securities and suffered losses on your investment, join our investigation now: Click here to join the investigation. Or for more information, contact Jim Baker at jimb@johnsonfistel.com or (619) 814-4471. There is no cost or obligation to you.

Background of the investigation On May 5, 2026, Primoris reported its first quarter 2026 financial results and updated its full-year outlook. Among other things, the Company disclosed revenue of $1.6 billion, down 5.4% compared to the prior-year period, and net income of $17.4 million, compared to $44.2 million in the prior-year period. Primoris also disclosed adjusted EBITDA of $60.5 million, down 39.1% compared to the first quarter of 2025, and adjusted EPS of $0.59, compared to $0.98 in the prior-year period. In addition, Primoris disclosed that revenue in its Energy segment decreased by $152.9 million, or 13.8%, compared to the prior-year period. The Company attributed the decrease primarily to lower renewable energy activity due to slower-than-anticipated starts of new projects, release of new work, and financial close associated with certain projects. Primoris further disclosed that Energy segment operating income decreased by $49.1 million, or 62.2%, compared to the prior-year period, due to decreased revenue and increased costs on certain renewable energy projects. The Company stated that these higher costs were driven in part by project redesign efforts, changes in project sequencing, labor productivity challenges, and unfavorable weather conditions. Energy gross profit as a percentage of revenue declined to 7.6%, compared to 10.7% in the prior-year period. Primoris also reduced its full-year 2026 outlook, including adjusted EPS guidance of $4.80 to $5.00, compared to prior guidance of $5.80 to $6.00, and adjusted EBITDA guidance of $480 million to $500 million, compared to prior guidance of $560 million to $580 million. Following this disclosure, Primoriss stock price declined sharply, damaging investors. In light of this disclosure, Johnson Fistel is investigating whether Primoris complied with the federal securities laws. If you suffered losses from your investment in Primoris stock, contact Johnson Fistel. About Johnson Fistel, PLLP | Securities Fraud &Investor Rights Johnson Fistel, PLLP is a nationally recognized shareholder-rights law firm with offices in California, New York, Georgia, Idaho, and Colorado. The firm represents individual and institutional investors in shareholder derivative and securities class action lawsuits and also assists foreign investors who purchased shares on U.S. exchanges. To learn more, visit www.johnsonfistel.com. Achievements In 2024, Johnson Fistel was ranked among the Top 10 Plaintiff Law Firms by ISS Securities Class Action Services, reflecting the firms effectiveness in advocating for investors and recovering approximately $90,725,000 for clients in cases where it served as lead or co-lead counsel. Attorney advertising. Past results do not guarantee future outcomes. Services may be performed by attorneys in any of our offices. Johnson Fistel, PLLP has paid for the dissemination of this promotional communication, and Frank J. Johnson is the attorney responsible for its content. Contact Johnson Fistel, PLLP 501 W. Broadway, Suite 800 San Diego, CA 92101 James Baker, Investor Relations or Frank J. Johnson, Esq. (619) 814-4471 jimb@johnsonfistel.comfjohnson@johnsonfistel.com
